desc:
Here, a big stone has been set in the middle of the East Road to mark the
meeting point of three of the four farthings in the Shire: the eastern, the
southern and the western ones. To the north there are some meadows and two
towns, but a large building prevents any step in that direction. To the south
only fields can be seen.
name: The Three-Farthings Stone

desc:
This crossroad lies in the middle of the plain. The East Road runs east and
west, passing through the Shire. There is a small path to the south and a large
road heads towards a village in the north. A sign can be seen at the northern
side of the crossroad.
name: A Crossroad

desc:
Here the East Road passes through the farmed plains that cover the land. While
there is a dark lane to the north, which leads to the bank of the river, the
road is otherwise monotonously straight. The earthy ground is covered by
innumerable tracks, made by passing hobbits, horses and carts.
name: The East Road

desc:
To the north, the road is bordered by a river which runs east-west and to the
south the road is bordered by some fields. Beyond the fields, a small green hill
is visible along with a few lonely trees and some farms. The outline of a small
town is barely discernible in the distant north-west.
name: The East Road

desc:
Here, the road is very muddy, making it easy to distinguish a set of footprints
heading towards the southern field. To the north a small river can be heard and
to the far south the plain slopes upwards forming a large hill.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road continues east-west across the Shire. It is evident from the well worn
tracks that this road is used by many of the inhabitants of the Shire. Parallel
to the road, at some distance to the north, a small river flows towards the
east. To the far south, beyond the fields, there are some hills.
name: The East Road

desc:
Here, a fork in the road branches to the north and leads to the the town of
Dwaling. Along this path, one can see the nearby river and fields. The East Road
continues to the east and west across the Shire.
name: A Fork

desc:
This road, built across the Shire, heads in an east-west direction. To the north
a small river overflows its bank and to the south there are some evenly spaced
trees, blocking the view in that direction.
name: The East Road

desc:
Just east of here there is a small village and to the west, several miles down
the road, there is a crossroad. To the north a small river has overflowed its
bank and to the far south the fields slope upwards forming a green hill.
name: The East Road

desc:
This small village is formed by some houses built on the sides of the East Road.
It is also used for travellers and adventurers often to take a rest since it has
a good inn. The Floating Log is just north of here, offering good food and
beverages.
name: Inside the Small Village of Frogmorton

desc:
You are in a small village built on the sides of the East Road. Some houses and
hobbits can be seen around. Although this place is not too populated, it has
quite a good inn, since many travellers and adventurers use it to take a rest:
The Floating Log Inn.
name: Inside the Small Village of Frogmorton

desc:
The East Road continues, passing through the Shire now. This land is peaceful
and its people friendly. You can count on a safe passage through it. To the
south a hobbit farm is built, and to the north the Water goes silently to join
with the Brandywine. To the west you see a small village.
name: The East Road

desc:
The East Road continues, passing through the Shire now. This land is peaceful
and its people friendly. You can count on a safe passage through it. To the
south a hobbit farm has been built, and to the north the Water goes silently to
join with the Brandywine.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road, that has crossed the most strange lands, is now running through a
quiet and peaceful region. One can follow the road east or west. To the north, a
stand of reeds makes it impossible to go there. To the south you see furrowed
fields behind a small fence. It seems easy to leap over it.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road, that is wandering through the most strange lands, is now crossing a
quiet and peaceful region. You can follow the road east or west. To the north, a
stand of reeds makes it impossible to go there. To the south you see furrowed
fields behind a small fence. It is easy to leap over it.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road is quite easy here. Everything seems peaceful and one could hardly
expect any hostility from the surroundings. To the north is a yard protected by
a fence. You can follow the way to the west or east.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road continues to the west and east. The region you are crossing now seems
very peaceful, and you can walk without being worried about danger. To the east
you see a small village of really little houses.
name: The East Road

desc:
The road now is crossing a small village formed by cute little houses of
hobbits. They are really small. All around you, you see the people of the
village doing their labors, walking from one place to another. The road goes to
the west and the east. North of here a little street goes through the houses.
name: Witfurrows

desc:
Surrounded by vast fields that sway softly in the gentle breeze, the road here
bends slightly with the sounds of villages in either direction.
name: The East Road

desc:
The old road winds from the southwest to the northeast, through flat, lush,
well-farmed terrain. Orchards, fields, pastures, and groves of oak and beech
line the road, dotted with round-doored farmhouses. To the northwest, beyond the
trees marking the valley of The Water, a large hill can be seen at a distance of
four or five miles.
name: The East Road

desc:
The East Road winds through the Shire, passing through the well-kept fields and
orchards. While the trees to the south block your vision, to the north the
outline of two nearby towns can be seen. To the east and west the monotony of
the road remains.
name: The East Road

desc:
You are standing in the centre of a small group of smials that form this tiny
village. The houses here are not quite as nice as those located in the rest of
the Shire.
name: Overhill Village

desc:
This smial is almost comparable to a simple hole in the ground. The furniture is
in disrepair, and the place seems fairly devoid of any signs of wealth. Even so,
the hobbits who live here take pride in themselves and keep the place fairly
clean.
name: A Poor Smial

desc:
The Water flows quietly under the wooden planks of the bridge. To your sides,
you can see the banks of the river covered by mud and reeds. You cannot leave
the river here.
name: Under the Bridge

desc:
The river floods toward the east. Far in the west there is some houses. It seems
that the river is going forever straight east. The north bank is unreachable
because of some sharp cliffs and on the south bank a small mill stands, powered
by the waters of the river.
name: The Water

desc:
This river flows calmly from its source somewhere in the west towards the east
where it should mix its waters with those of the river Baranduin. A small bend
can be seen in the east, where the river flows along a small town. To the north,
there are some cultivated fields, also there are some sharp cliffs which prevent
to entry to those northern lands. On the south bank is a small road that crosses
the countryside.
name: The Water

desc:
This is a river bend. Southwards there is a small lake. On its south bank some
buildings stand, indicating the place of a village. Otherwise the land,
cultivated, is wholly flat. Its monotony is broken only by small waves of the
ground, and some lonely trees.
name: The Water

desc:
This small lake, north of the village of Bywater, is really quiet: only a few
billows prevent the watery surface to be a perfect mirror. To the north there is
the Water entering the small lake, and to east it flows away.
name: Bywater Pool

desc:
Just between the Bywater Road and the Water stands this busy mill yard. The loud
sounds of the mill machinery can be heard from the eastern building, the
property of Ted Sandyman the miller, a man not known to be fond of strangers
prowling about his land.
name: A Mill Yard

desc:
The sounds of the mill overpower all other noises. To the north there is the
machinery working with the waters of the river, milling grain into meal for
consumption by the hobbits who live in the Shire. Ted Sandyman, the owner of the
place, must be nearby as he doesn't like to leave his building unsupervised.
name: A Mill

desc:
The road makes a bend here: it comes from Hobbiton, a few miles away in the
west, and goes to Bywater which lies on the southern bank of the small pool that
lies just here. Before that pool there is a small bank with hundreds of small
stones scattered about it. A small coppice of chestnut trees stands between the
road and the northern river.
name: Bywater Road

desc:
This road is made of earth as hard as stone. It is one of the most used way in
the whole Shire as it binds the small town of Hobbiton, just west of here, and
the big village of Bywater, a few miles in the south-east. The road goes
east-west, parallel to the Water that flows just north of here, after a small
mill. South of the road, there is some fields, divided by hedgerows, and not far
the dark line of the East Road can be seen.
name: Bywater Road

desc:
This field, uncultivated, is a grazing field. Grass is the best food for the
cows of Bywater's farmers. Just south of here a big road is running eastwards-
westwards and there are two villages in the neighbourhood: Bywater in the east
and Hobbiton far in the northwest. A large wooden building prevents walking
eastwards.
name: A Green Meadow

desc:
This is a quite small village. The streets are bordered by lots of chestnut
trees. The houses are either little constructions on the ground with round doors
and windows, or dug-in houses, which seem to be very comfortable excavations.
The only building which seems to be interesting is called the Green Dragon Inn.
name: Bywater

desc:
This is a quite small village. The streets are bordered by lots of chestnut
trees. The houses are either little constructions on the ground with round doors
and windows, or dug-in houses, which seem to be very comfortable excavations. To
the south you see a street leading to a main road. To the north you see a large
pool, and to the west a road leaves town and winds through the fields toward a
distant hill.
name: Bywater

desc:
The Bywater Road bypasses the pool of the Water that lies, quiet, north of the
famous village of Bywater. The first houses of it are less than one mile east of
here. A lonely inn, in an old wooden house, stands on the west of the road; its
stables are a large wooden building, south of the road. Some ponies' neighings
can be heard.
name: By an Inn on the Bywater Road

desc:
This is one of the finest inns of the Shire, famous for its beer and food. It is
usually a fairly busy place, with many hobbits relaxing here after a hard day's
work. Many a fine story is spun here by the local storytellers. A wooden door
leads south to the guest rooms, where an adventurer can rest his or her weary
bones, for a small fee of course. A small sign is nailed upon the bar.
name: The Ivy Bush Inn

desc:
This room is very quiet: even the merry sounds of the northern inn do not make
their way here. A big door stands open to the west and through it one can see
the many other doors leading to the guest rooms of the reception.
name: The Reception

desc:
This large room is filled with lots of tables. The fire sparkles softly in the
left corner. The old bar stands on the right side. Hobbits are chatting here,
and all of them have some empty cups of beer laying on their table. A small sign
stands upright on the bar.
name: The Green Dragon

desc:
This small brisky river floods eastwards. The waters are clear and one can see
the bottom of the river even if the current is strong. The dark lane of a road
follows the river in the south, and the northern dam is filled by many sharp
cliffs, blocking the passage.
name: The Water

desc:
This river is flooding eastwards. Parallel to it there is a dark line to the
south, probably a path or a road. As much the south coast of the river is calm
and covered of grass, the north coast is filled with many sharp cliffs,
preventing every movements in that direction.
name: The Water

desc:
This small river is flooding eastwards. The inhabitants of this country call it
'The Water'. South of here one can see a road and west is a small lake, but
there are so many sharp cliffs along the north bank of this river that one
cannot see any further.
name: The Water

desc:
This field is on the south bank of a briskly flowing river. The ground is very
muddy. Not far in the west there is a small village and just south of here there
is the lane of a road. Far in the south, after lots of fields, some green hills
are standing, breaking the monotony of the plain.
name: Bank of the Water

desc:
Between the northly river and the southly road, the ground is very muddy. Just
east of here there is a small construction and further some houses stand on the
plain. The south part of it is covered by lots of rectangular fields.
name: Bank of the Water

desc:
The river, which runs northly of here, should often become greater and flood a
part of the plain. Here the ground is very muddy, indicating that great amount
of water run here not so long ago. Southly there is a big road.
name: Bank of the Water

desc:
This field is covered by a thick dark green grass. Nevertheless the ground is
very spongious due to the amount of water the near river let periodically run
here when some flooding occurs. A dark earth road lies just south of here, and a
small bridge can be seen over the river northeast of here.
name: Bank of the Water

desc:
This river floods cold from the west toward the east. There a small bridge
stands over it. Not far in the south there is the dark line of a parallel road
leading to the bridge. The north coast of the river is covered by sharp cliffs.
name: The Water

desc:
This part of the dam of the Water is not cultivated. The ground is sandy and
therefore any walk is harder here. Lots of bushes grow around this place, making
the road difficult to reach. A big tree, an oak, can be seen west from here and
the river floating just south of here makes a lot of noise.
name: Bushes

desc:
This place is not cultivated. There is only a huge tree growing here. It is so
huge that its stems are covering the ground all around. Its canopy is so high
and so dense, that daylight is filtered and the main colour one can see is dark
green. The flat, smooth bark that covers this tree is too slippery for climbing.
name: Under a Big Tree

desc:
This northwest-southeast road goes along a river. It seems to disappear in the
north in an endless somewhat hilly green plain. Not so far in the southeast
there is a small bridge over a river. The north bank of the river is filled by
sharp rocks.
name: A Large Path

desc:
This field, lying among the other fields wholly covering this wide plain, is
cultivated. Lots of furrows are covering it from the east to the west. At each
corner stands a tree or a big stone, delineating the property of each hobbit
farmer.
name: A Field near the Path

desc:
This place, just westward of an important path, is the end of one of the fields
of the Shire. The furrows stop suddenly and a big lonely tree marks the end of
the field.
name: At the Lonely Tree

desc:
This large dirty path connects Dwaling to the East Road. The path runs from the
north-west to the south-east. This area of the Shire is very peaceful. By
listening carefully one can hear a nearby river and some birds singing in the
distance.
name: A Large Path

desc:
The path here makes a gentle turn from the south-east to the north-east, heading
towards the hills of Scary which rise in the distance and toward the village of
Dwaling beyond. The countryside around is very serene and calm.
name: A Large Path

desc:
This well-trod path slopes gently upwards as it heads north-east towards the
hills of Scary. The surrounding countryside lies calmly around you, trees gently
rustling in the soft breeze.
name: A Large Path

desc:
This field is cultivated. The furrows leads north-south and some of the plants
begin to grow : you see here and there some green twigs growing. South of here
there are some trees and a medium-sized roads runs eastward of here.
name: A Field near a Road

desc:
This dirty road, not much larger than a path, is connecting the hobbit village
of Dwaling to the East Road. It mainly goes from the northwest to the southeast
through a very peaceful land, only disturbed by some birds songs and a very
faint noise of water.
name: A Large Path

desc:
This road is connecting Dwaling to the East Road. It is somewhat dirty and
hundreds of tracks can be seen on it. It goes from the northwest to the
southeast. The land around is very peaceful, silent except some birds and a very
distant noise of water flooding.
name: A Large Path

desc:
This is a large path that climbs gently into the hills of Scary which leads
towards the village of Dwaling that lies unseen in the distance. All around
roams the wildlife of the countryside, free of worry or care, amidst the
renowned foliage of the Shire.
name: A Large Path

desc:
This large path leads northwards and eastwards from the East Road towards the
hills of Scary and the fishing village of Dwaling that lies beyond. The
surrounding countryside is quiet and calm and the foliage is quite splendid,
supporting the reputation of the Shire gardeners.
name: A Large Path

desc:
The road narrows a bit here as it curves its way northwards into the hills of
Scary. Less travelled than the path to Dwaling proper, the road still remains
hard underfoot and is easy to travel on.
name: A Large Path

desc:
To the north the road enters a crossroad at the foot of the hills of Scary. The
countryside is as serene as ever, however, a more silent stillness seems to come
from the hills to the east. Yet this is the peaceful Shire, and the grass around
moves slowly under the light wind.
name: A Large Path

desc:
Here a large path from the south, coming up from the old east road, joins a wide
path from Scary to the east to form the path to Dwaling northwards that skirts
the rising hills of Scary. The road seems to be well travelled and is
comfortable underfoot.
name: Crossroad to Dwaling

desc:
You have reached a flat hilltop on the north-western edge of a range of hills
known in the Shire as the hills of Scary. The tall, untrampled grass and wild
shrubbery tell tales of desertion and isolation. Gazing outwards, you see an
endless expanse of grassland to the north and what looks like a village some way
off to the north-west. A narrow trail winds down the northern slope here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have climbed up on one of the higher ridges of the hills of Scary. Wild
grass and shrubbery cover the ground as elsewhere. Gazing northwards, you see an
endless expanse of grassland extending all the way to the horizon. The northern
slope at this point is too steep to descend.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You stand on a higher hilltop at the western end of a range of hills known in
the Shire as the hills of Scary. Gazing outwards, you make out a village far to
the north-west. The western slope is too steep to descend, but you can see that
the hills end a short distance north and south.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have reached a flat hilltop on the south-western edge of a range of hills
known in the Shire as the hills of Scary. The tall, untrampled grass and wild
shrubbery tell tales of desertion and isolation. Gazing outwards, you see what
looks like a village far to the north-west and a cluster of farmed lands a
little way south. A narrow trail winds down the southern slope here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You travel on a wide path which winds along the foot of the hills of Scary. Old
criss-cross tracks of wheels and hooves much like those seen on the path to
Dwaling suggest some of the carts from Dwaling pass this way. A small trail
leads up the slope on your north. You can follow the path east or west.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
This wide path narrows a bit as it winds along the hills here, joining a
crossroads to the west. The hardened road seems to bear the burden of many
Dwaling carts. To the east the road leads into the small village of Scary.
name: A Narrowing Path

desc:
You stand in an open field of grass bordered on the north and east by a wide
path. Wild flowers growing haphazardly here and there lend their bright colours
to the picturesque scene. A wooden fence blocks travel to the south.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You travel on a wide path which winds along the foot of the hills of Scary. Old
criss-cross tracks of wheels and hooves much like those seen on the path to
Dwaling suggest some of the carts from Dwaling pass this way. The path makes a
turn to the north and east here. A large field opens up to your west, while a
wooden fence blocks passage south.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
You travel on a wide path which winds along the foot of the hills of Scary. Old
criss-cross tracks of wheels and hooves much like those seen on the path to
Dwaling suggest some of the carts from Dwaling pass this way. The path makes a
turn to the south and west around the hills here.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
You are on a southern ridge of the hills of Scary. Wild grass and shrubbery
cover the ground as elsewhere. Gazing southwards, you see a cluster of farmed
lands a little way off. Nearer at hand, a wide path winds its way to the village
of Scary just at the foot of these hills, but there is no way down from here. A
steep, unclimbable hill rises to your east.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
Here the hills seem to envelope you entirely as they rise and fall all around. A
sudden feeling of isolation and loneliness grips you, though you know you are
still in the Shire and the exit from these hills is not far off. You look
around, and are a little reassured by the familiar sight of grass and shrubs.
Still, you start to wonder if the village of Scary got its name from these
hills.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
This appears to be where the farmers of Scary store their crops after harvest.
Some remnant stalks in one corner indicate where the unthreshed wheat is kept
temporarily in rainy weather. Sacks of grain, potatoes and mushrooms stand
neatly stacked against one wall, while dry hay is piled against another.
name: A Small Barn

desc:
You are at one end of a mushroom field. Narrow paths have been left between neat
rows of mushrooms to allow for walking. A wooden fence on the northern edge of
the field keeps out trespassers. You see a small wooden building on your left.
name: Mushroom Field

desc:
You are at one end of a mushroom field. Narrow paths have been left between neat
rows of mushrooms to allow for walking. A wooden fence wraps around the northern
and eastern edges of the field to keep out trespassers.
name: Mushroom Field

desc:
You guess that this field is used for cultivating wheat. Harvesting seems to be
just over, for the field is bare save for stray stalks of wheat lying here and
there. On the south a wooden fence keeps out trespassers. A small wooden
building stands to your west.
name: Wheat Field

desc:
You see various farming tools and implements neatly arranged in labelled wooden
boxes stacked against the western wall. In the eastern wall, a small window
looks out on the furrowed fields. A round wooden table and some chairs set
beside the window suggest that the shed doubles as the farmers' rest area.
name: A Toolshed

desc:
You guess that this field is used for cultivating wheat. Harvesting seems to be
just over, for the field is bare save for stray stalks of wheat lying here and
there. On the south a wooden fence keeps out trespassers.
name: Wheat Field

desc:
You travel on a wide path which winds along the foot of the hills of Scary. Old
criss-cross tracks of wheels and hooves much like those seen on the path to
Dwaling suggest some of the carts from Dwaling pass this way. The path leads
east and west here. On your south a wooden fence borders the path.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
The path leaves the foot of the hills at this point and maps a straight course
east to the village of Scary. This part of it appears to be kept in better
repair, perhaps due to the proximity of the village. The same criss-cross tracks
that are so plainly visible to the north are much fainter here. To the south and
west, a wooden fence bends round the path.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
The village of Scary is now clearly visible to the east, and you can see that
the path you are on runs right through its middle. This part of the path seems
to be kept in constant repair, for old tracks can barely be seen. A wooden fence
runs the length of the path to your south.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
Here neat rows of turnip shoots lie between equally neat furrows. The shoots
look young, and you decide against digging up a few for supper. A wooden fence
on the north and south keeps out trespassers.
name: Turnip Field

desc:
Potato tendrils run all over the field here, and you have to walk with extreme
caution so as not to step on them. You see that some attempt has been made to
impose order on the tendrils by the placement of furrows, but even the hobbits'
propensity for neatness has to give way to nature. To the north and south a
wooden fence keeps out trespassers.
name: Potato Field

desc:
This part of the farmed fields is used for growing vegetables. The field is
sub-divided into smaller plots by furrows and footpaths. In some plots, you see
green shoots only poking their tips out of the soil, while in others, leafy,
delicious-looking vegetables tempt you. Yet other plots are empty. A wooden
fence surrounds the field on the north, east and south.
name: Vegetable Fields

desc:
You are in a grassy field bordered by wooden fences west and south. A neat row
of low hedges separates the field from the village of Scary on the north, and a
wide path on the east. The hedges are low enough to jump over, but there is an
opening in the northern end from which a small muddy path leads to the large
well in the middle of the field.
name: At a Well

desc:
Like all hobbit villages, the village of Scary is well-ordered with its neat
rows of houses on both sides of the wide path which runs right through its
middle. This end of the village appears to be poorer than its neighbours to the
west, for the houses are really small and some have no pretense of a garden
even. A few old carts lean outside some of the houses.
name: Scary

desc:
Like all hobbit villages, the village of Scary is well-ordered with its neat
rows of houses on both sides of the wide path which runs right through its
middle. You can see that this is a poorer part of the Shire, as the houses are
generally small and the gardens just bits of tended ground. On the south, a
wooden fence has been erected around some farmland.
name: Scary

desc:
You travel on a wide path which winds along the foot of the hills of Scary. Old
criss-cross tracks of wheels and hooves much like those seen on the path to
Dwaling suggest some of the carts from Dwaling pass this way. The path makes a
turn to the west and south here. Large open fields are visible on your north and
east.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
You are in an open field of grass bordered on the south and west by a wide path,
and on the north by a sheer hillside. Wild flowers growing haphazardly here and
there lend their bright colours to the picturesque scene.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are in a well-tended field which extends to the east. The grass here is of
even height everywhere, the flowers and trees grow in neat little patches. You
guess that this is the party field of Scary, where all important events are
held. To your north and west are more fields, but they do not look as well-cared
for.
name: Well-tended Field

desc:
You are in a well-tended field which extends to the west. The grass here is of
even height everywhere, the flowers and trees grow in neat little patches. You
guess that this is the party field of Scary, where all important events are
held. North and east the field ends at low hedges which surround some private
gardens. Small lanes lead straight through these gardens to bright round doors
set in the hillside.
name: Well-tended Field

desc:
You have entered an extremely comfortable-looking hobbit hole. From the size and
furnishing of this place, you guess that the owner is much wealthier than his
neighbours in Scary. A small round window set in the western wall looks out on
the garden, and a big fireplace sits against the eastern wall.
name: A Hobbit Hole

desc:
You are in an unremarkable small field off a wide path to your south. On all
other sides, the rising hills of Scary look imposing as they fence you in. You
notice an uncovered hole in the northern hillside, which looks barely big enough
for an average-sized hobbit to squeeze through.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are struck by the contrast of this field against the one you just left
south. The tall, uneven grass has apparently never been cut, and the flowers and
trees have apparently been left to grow anywhere they please. On three sides the
rising hills of Scary fence you in, so that your only exit is back south.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You find yourself on a narrow ledge carved out of the side of a steep hill which
looms high on your south. The ledge continues to the east, while a safer-looking
plateau beckons to you from the west. North is a sheer drop down to something
shiny. You wonder if there is water down below.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
From the shape of this terrain, you can almost imagine yourself to be on the
bottom of a gigantic furrow. Sheer hillsides loom to your north and south, while
the relatively level ground you are on dips slightly from west to east. The
grass appears greener, the trees taller and more numerous, perhaps due to the
little stream running its course through here.
name: By a Small Stream

desc:
The feeling of insecurity heightens as you continue along the narrow ridge which
remains level for some distance to the east and west. Here the hills of Scary
end in a sharp incline on your north. Looking down the southern edge, you detect
a faint glint as of a shiny surface below, but there is no way to descend from
this point.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have climbed up on one of the higher ridges of the hills of Scary. Wild
grass and shrubbery cover the ground as elsewhere. Gazing northwards, you see an
endless expanse of grassland extending all the way to the horizon. The northern
slope at this point is too steep to descend.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
Here the hills seem to envelope you entirely as they rise and fall all around. A
sudden feeling of isolation and loneliness grips you, though you know you are
still in the Shire and the exit from these hills is not far off. You look
around, and are a little reassured by the familiar sight of grass and shrubs.
Still, you start to wonder if the village of Scary got its name from these
hills.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a low plateau walled by higher hills both east and west. Wild grass
and shrubbery cover the ground as elsewhere. To the north you see what looks
like a huge pit with a small spring in its centre, while on your south the hills
end in a steep drop down to a large grassy field. A little further south from
the field, you can see the village of Scary with smoke spiralling out of its
numerous chimneys.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have descended into what can only be described as a huge pit in the hills of
Scary, for rising hills surround you on three sides. On your north, the hillside
is so sheer that it forms an unscalable wall, though the slopes on your south
and west are climbable. The grass here looks greener, and trees grow in place of
the stunted shrubs elsewhere, perhaps due to the proximity of the small spring.
To the east the ground seems to dip slightly, as water from the spring flows off
in that direction in a little stream.
name: At a Spring

desc:
You feel a little unsafe on this narrow ridge which remains level for some
distance to the east, but dips and broadens to the west. Here the hills of Scary
end in a sharp incline on your north. Looking down the southern edge, you detect
a faint glint as of a shiny surface below, but there is no way to descend from
this point.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You stand on a low hilltop on the northern edge of a range of hills known in the
Shire as the hills of Scary. The tall, untrampled grass and wild shrubbery tell
tales of desertion and isolation. Gazing northwards, you see an endless expanse
of grassland extending to the horizon. A narrow trail winds down the northern
slope here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
Here the hills seem to envelope you entirely as they rise and fall all around. A
sudden feeling of isolation and loneliness grips you, though you know you are
still in the Shire and the exit from these hills is not far off. You look
around, and are a little reassured by the familiar sight of grass and shrubs.
Still, you start to wonder if the village of Scary got its name from these
hills.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
The homogeneity of the hills break a little, to your relief. Though wild grass
and shrubs surround you still, the ground slopes down on your east, and you can
see what looks like a small spring a short way off.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have climbed up on one of the higher ridges of the hills of Scary. Wild
grass and shrubbery cover the ground as elsewhere. Gazing northwards, you see an
endless expanse of grassland extending all the way to the horizon. The northern
slope at this point is too steep to descend.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You feel a little unsafe on this narrow ridge which remains level for some
distance to the west, but dips and broadens to the east. Here the hills of Scary
end in a sharp incline on your north. Looking down the southern edge, you detect
a faint glint as of a shiny surface below, but there is no way to descend from
this point.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You stand on a low hilltop on the northern edge of a range of hills known in the
Shire as the hills of Scary. The tall, untrampled grass and wild shrubbery tell
tales of desertion and isolation. Gazing northwards, you see an endless expanse
of grassland extending to the horizon. A narrow trail winds down the northern
slope here, while a tangle of tree-tops is visible a little way below the
southern edge. You wonder what may be hidden under, but there is no way down in
that direction.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are in a beautiful valley which teems with life - the life of trees and
flowers and grass and small animals. Yet you feel a strange loneliness and
sadness here, as if the valley itself is mourning for something lost to it. The
valley continues to your east, where the trees seem to grow denser. A small
waterfall cascades down the sheer hillside on your west.
name: A Valley

desc:
Here the stream comes to the end of its short course, as the water cascades down
the sudden drop on your east. Sheer hillsides still block your way north or
south, but the beautiful valley you can see below to your east entices you to
explore. You wonder if you can go down that way.
name: By a Small Stream

desc:
You find yourself on a narrow ledge carved out of the side of a steep hill which
looms high on your south. The ledge continues to the west, but broadens as it
slopes up east to a flat hilltop. North is a sheer drop down to something shiny.
You wonder if there is water down below.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a flat hilltop deep in the folds of the hills of Scary, where no
external sights or sounds intrude. Everywhere you look, you see grass, shrubs
and more hills. The ground slopes up sharply on your south, but is still
climbable. On the north is a sheer drop to a tangle of tree-tops a little way
below. You wonder what may be hidden under, but there is no way down from here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are deep in the folds of the hills of Scary, where no external sights or
sounds intrude. Everywhere you look, you see grass, shrubs and more hills. The
slopes in all directions are climbable, but they seem to lead to identical
terrain.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You are on a high hilltop deep in the folds of the hills of Scary, where no
external sights or sounds intrude. Everywhere you look, you see grass, shrubs
and more hills. In all directions except east, the downward slope is too steep
to attempt a descent. The terrain to your east looks the same from here.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
You have entered an extremely comfortable-looking hobbit hole. The long hallway
leads off into the back, and doors open from it at intervals. The smell of
pastries and cakes wafts in from one or a few of the doors. You briefly wonder
what is baking, but the laden table here is of more interest for now. A big
fireplace sits against the northern wall.
name: A Hobbit Hole

desc:
The village of Scary is clearly visible to the west, and you can see that the
path you are on runs right through its middle. Small stones and rocks litter the
path here, making it on the whole rather uneven and unpleasant to walk on. Off
the eastern side, a smaller albeit rockier path leads to a large quarry. A sheer
hillside borders the path on your north.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
This part of the path is littered with small stones and rocks, making it rather
uneven and unpleasant to walk on. West of here a row of low hedges separates the
path from a field, but you can easily jump over it. A small fence blocks your
way to the south, while the path continues east and north.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
You have come to the southern end of the hills of Scary. Here the terrain seems
rockier, and there is a noticeable thinning of the grass which covers the
ground. Looking down the steep southern slope, you see what appears to be a
quarry, but there is no way down.
name: Hills of Scary

desc:
Here the path makes a turn to the south and west away from the hills. Small
stones and rocks are scattered all over, making it on the whole rather uneven
and unpleasant to walk on. Off your north, you see a large quarry, and a smaller
but rockier path approaches it from this side.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
You are in one of the large quarries for which the village of Scary is known.
Evidence of recent excavation is everywhere, but you do not see any hobbit at
work now. Two loaded carts of stones lean against one side waiting to be brought
away. You see a wooden partition on your east.
name: Quarry

desc:
These are good fields and on them grow nice and healthy vegetables. All around,
except west, a fence surrounds this place. Over it, you see a wide path to the
east.
name: Vegetable Fields

desc:
The path continues north of here to the steep hills that lie beyond. To the
south lies the Witfurrows, and to the west a small fence prevents you from going
into some fields of vegetables. East you can see big grassy fields.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
This path is going west to a little house. North a fence goes along the path and
over it you can see some fields of vegetables. To the east is the wide path that
goes north-south.
name: Path through the Fields

desc:
This well maintained path crosses the big fields to the north of the Old East
Road and west of the Brandywine. All around you, you see them covered by green
and tall grass. Some houses can be seen in the distance. The road leads north to
the hills and south to the Witfurrows.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
You stand in a large field, surrounded by grass and small trees. Small, yellow
flowers and other plants grow here and there. The field is flat, and seems to be
used for grazing.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You stand in a large field, surrounded by grass and small trees. Small, yellow
flowers and other plants grow here and there. The field is flat, and seems to be
used for grazing.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You stand in a large field, surrounded by grass and small trees. Small, yellow
flowers and other plants grow here and there. The field is flat, and seems to be
used for grazing.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You stand in a large field, surrounded by grass and small trees. Small, yellow
flowers and other plants grow here and there. The field is flat, and seems to be
used for grazing.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You stand in a large field, surrounded by grass and small trees. Small, yellow
flowers and other plants grow here and there. The field is flat, and seems to be
used for grazing.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You stand in a large field, surrounded by grass and small trees. Small, yellow
flowers and other plants grow here and there. The field is flat, and seems to be
used for grazing.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
This field lies in the middle of a cultivated plain, covering the northern part
of the Shire. The lands surrounding are flat and cultivated, except for a small
red hill not very far to the east. Farther to the south, one can see two lines
crossing eastwards-westwards; one is a river, the other a road.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
This is the northern bank of a river called 'The Water'. Hobbits, as they are
well-known farmers, have even planted something here. So the top of this dam is
covered by some furrows. Far in the south-east, there are some houses belonging
to a small village.
name: A Cultivated Dam

desc:
You are floating on a small tributary of the Brandywine. The river flows to the
east. Small trees and bushes crowd close to the bank here. The banks are steep
and you see no good place to land.
name: The Water

desc:
You are floating on the north branch of The Water. The river comes together
again further to the east. You seem to be surrounded by soft grassy fields
everywhere you look.
name: The Water

desc:
The river is surrounded by grassy fields in every direction. To the east the
water continues until meeting the Brandywine, somewhere in the east.
name: The Water

desc:
You are floating on a small tributary of the Brandywine. Here the course turns
to the south, to meet another branch of The Water. Everywhere around the river
you see big fields of thick grass.
name: The Water

desc:
You are floating on a small tributary of the Brandywine. The river flows to the
east. Here it forks in two branches, leaving a narrow portion of land between
them. You can follow the river to the north or south.
name: The Water

desc:
This small island is surrounded by The Water. In the centre of a grassy plain
you see a solitary gnarled blackthorne tree. The place seems unnaturally quiet
and you feel the urge to walk softly and whisper as if in a church.
name: A Small Island

desc:
Here is the reunion of the two branches of the river. Together, the water
continues to the east, to meet the Brandywine.
name: The Water

desc:
The river flows gently to the east to meet the Brandywine. To the north you can
go into the fields leaving the river. To the south a dense stand of reeds
prevents you from taking that direction.
name: The Water

desc:
The river flows gently to the east to join its confluence with the Brandywine.
It has just divided into two branches, but they get together further to the
east. You are on the southern part.
name: The Water

desc:
The river flows gently through the fields of the Shire. To the north, you see a
tiny island trapped between two forks of The Water. To the south, you can see
the East Road, and to the east, the water continues until it meets the
Brandywine, somewhere in the distance.
name: The Water

desc:
You are now floating on a small tributary of the Brandywine. Here the course
turns to the north, to meet another flow of The Water. To the south you see the
East Road beyond the bank.
name: The Water

desc:
You stand in a small inn, which seems to be a second home for some people. There
is even a small bed with a pipe lying next to it, and a few hobbits sitting
nearby discussing everyday topics. A carved wooden sign hangs here.
name: The Floating Log

desc:
The river flows gently to the east to meet the Brandywine. To the north is a big
field that appears to be a yard of some kind. You can go there, but south a
dense stand of reeds prevents you from going that direction.
name: The Water

desc:
The river flows gently to the east, to meet the Brandywine. To the north you see
some fields of vegetables, but a fence on the border of the river prevents you
from going there.
name: The Water

desc:
The river is now passing by the small village of Witfurrows, built on its shore.
You can see that the houses there are small and could hardly be used by a human
being. A ford forces you to leave the river if you want to keep sailing.
name: The Water

desc:
Well maintained and frequently used, it is hard to say if this is really a path
or a little road. It was built to make it possible to easily walk between
Witfurrows and the hills to the north. South, a ford crosses over The Water,
into a village.
name: A Wide Path

desc:
The ford is formed by strong wood planks placed over The Water's surface.
Through it presence, the river can be crossed and thus it it is possible to make
for the fields and the hills to the north along a wide path. South is the
village of Witfurrows.
name: Budge Ford

desc:
This street was built to join the East Road with the ford crossing The Water.
There are hobbits everywhere you look, walking without care. They seem used to
the passing of travellers and your presence does not seem to worry them.
name: Road through the Village

desc:
This village, built on one side of the road, is formed by little houses of
hobbits. To the north is The Water, and beyond are the hills in the distance.
All around you see people walking doing their daily labours. The hobbits have
always been a peaceful race, and this village proves it.
name: The Village of Witfurrows

desc:
Here can be found different kinds of animals that the hobbits use, ponies are
most of them, but there are some cows and sheep too. To the north runs The
Water, and east is the village of Witfurrows.
name: A Timber-Yard

desc:
You are standing in the middle of a big field of green and thick grass. You can
see several different kinds of flowers, too, and some trees. To the north, you
can see the East Road, and to the south the fields continue into the distance.
name: Green Fields

desc:
In every direction you look, an endless field extends, covered by green and
thick grass. Lonely trees can be seen in different places and wild flowers of
mixed colors give some life to this land.
name: Green Fields

desc:
In every direction you look, an endless field extends, covered by green and
thick grass. Lonely trees can be seen in different places and wild flowers of
mixed colors give some life to this land.
name: Green Fields

desc:
This is a large and flat field. The grass is not too tall and some flowers can
be found. To the north you see a little path going along a fence. To the east is
a small road going north. West is another fence preventing you from entering
into some fields of vegetables. South you see The Water.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The ground slopes slightly downwards here. Some trees around make shadows on the
soil. The animals seem to like this place, a true oasis for the wild life amidst
the cultivated Shire. A small hole can be seen between two big rocks.
name: A Slight Depression

desc:
Some lonely trees cover the plain here, letting the wildlife survive in the
cultivated plain. The sun plays with the leaves of the trees, making coloured
spots on the soft green ground. A small road makes a bend south- west of here
and a strange red hill stands eastwards.
name: Some Scattered Trees

desc:
The Hobbits, as good farmers, have colonized almost all the lands of the Shire.
Even this bank by the river is cultivated. While this field, next to the river,
has fresh furrows, it is not obvious what will be grown here. Nevertheless, the
field seems well defined with the river to the south, the road to the west and a
strange red hill to the north.
name: A Cultivated Bank of the River

desc:
The path winds to the west towards Dwaling while to the south the path crosses
the Water as it heads towards the Old East Road. To the north and east there are
the cultivated fields of the hobbits. The sound of the river can be heard over
the chirping birds.
name: A Large Path

desc:
This narrow tunnel is very dry. It is basically just a small round corridor dug
from the soft ground amidst some big stones which prevent the passage from
collapsing and burying its inhabitants. The smell is wild but not bad at all.
The foxes appear to be good hunters as a few hen feathers on the ground can
attest to.
name: Foxes' Den

desc:
This small wooden bridge crosses the small river known as the Water. There are
signs that the river has frequently flowed over the bridge as well as under it,
but nevertheless it doesn't seem dangerous to cross the Water using it. It
should be easy to reach the surface of the Water from here. The path continues
through fields to the north and towards a distant crossing in the south.
name: A Bridge

desc:
This is a small road which heads towards Dwaling. A small bridge lies to the
north and the East Road is not far in the south. This area of the Shire is very
serene. Only the songs of birds and the noise of the river can be heard from
here.
name: A Large Path

desc:
You are on a small river flowing from the west to the east. You see a small
bridge at some distance to the west and a road to the south. All looks so
beautiful from here!
name: The Water

desc:
This small river floods briskly from the west to east. There is a small wooden
bridge at some distance in the west and a large road in the south where a large
flat cultivated plains lies. Far in the north, the ground slopes somewhat and
ends in some low hills.
name: The Water

desc:
This field, uncultivated, lies on the south bank of a briskly flowing river. The
ground is somewhat muddy, but the grass is dense. There is a large road just
south of here and one can see a endless plain south of it.
name: On the South Bank of the Water

desc:
This field, very well kept, belongs to the farm that can be seen not far to the
east. North of here, a small road runs east-west. To the west there are a few
lonely, big trees protecting the place there from the burning sun. The field
continues south of here.
name: A Field near Some Trees

desc:
This farm field is just south of a big road which runs east-west. The farm house
is just east of here. South of here the plain begins to slope upwards forming
some green hills in the distance. West of here there are some fields with a few
lonely trees.
name: Near a Farm

desc:
This land is used for cultivation and is all furrowed. Healthy plants grow here
and there. To the south you can see a pigpen, and to the north you can see the
East Road beyond a fence.
name: Furrowed Fields

desc:
You are in a small farm. In fact, everything is small here since it is a place
of hobbits. To the north you see a small fence that can be easily jumped, and
beyond the Old East Road. To the south you see a small tool shed.
name: Small Hobbit Farm

desc:
This land is used for cultivation and is all furrowed. Healthy plants grow here
and there. To the north, you see the East Road going through the Shire and its
peaceful fields.
name: Furrowed Fields

desc:
This land is used for cultivation and is all furrowed. Healthy plants grow here
and there. To the north, you see the Old East Road going through the Shire and
it's peaceful fields.
name: Furrowed Fields

desc:
You are in a small farm. In fact, everything is small here since it is a place
of hobbits. To the south you see a little house with a round door; it must be
the home of the farmer. To the north you see the Old East Road.
name: Small Hobbit Farm

desc:
Here the ground slopes up to the east, and you wonder if there is an exit in
that direction. The tunnel seems to be slightly wider at this point, for the
walls are not so threateningly close. Or is it just a trick of the mind?
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
You walk warily on, following the twists and turns of the tiny tunnel. With
every step, the smooth mud walls seem to edge ever closer, threatening to close
in on you.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
You appear to have come to a dead end. As you stare at the walls they almost
seem to come alive, as if trying to move toward you, closing in upon you. But
that can only be the workings of your imagination. However, it remains clear
that the only way out of this place is to the north.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
You walk warily on, following the twists and turns of the tiny tunnel. With
every step, the smooth mud walls seem to edge ever closer, threatening to close
in on you.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
You walk warily on, following the twists and turns of the tiny tunnel. With
every step, the smooth mud walls seem to edge ever closer, threatening to close
in on you.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
It seems that you are at a dead end. All about you the inky blackness of the
cold stone walls seems to close in on you, but it is just a trick of the eye.
However, the fact remains that the only way out of this place seems to be to the
north.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
To your disappointment, you appear to have come to a dead end. The smooth,
unbroken mud walls seem to mock you for believing they would open up to let you
out, and your dancing shadows seem to laugh silently at you. There seems to be
no choice but to backtrack.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
You are under a wooden bridge, which is quite new... The Water is running under
you from the west towards the east. The river is here quite under a wooden
bridge, which is not very old, although some sign of moisture indicates it has
been already flooded several times. The Water is running from the west towards
the east, and it seems possible to climb on the bridge from here.
name: Under the Bridge

desc:
This field lies near a small road which runs through the Shire, just north of
here. The field is well-kept and divided in small parcels limited by a single
stone at each end. In the south, there is a bigger field and the brown line of
an earth path can be seen in the east.
name: Well-farmed Field

desc:
This narrow path winds through the fields, giving the farmers access to those
fields far from the East Road in the north. The path is rarely used and has been
overgrown by weeds.
name: A North-South Path

desc:
Most of the inhabitants of the Shire are farmers. This field, near a crossroad,
is cultivated, as is the remainder of the plain. To the far south, there is a
forest covered hill, and to the north lies the dark bank of a river.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
This field, laying south of the East Road, is very well-kept. The farmer who
owns it must therefore give it some value. Nevertheless it does not seem to have
taken any precautions to protect his crops.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
This field is very strange. For while most of the cultivated fields on this
plain are covered with east-west furrows, this field has north-south furrows.
While this breaks the monotony, it is quite confusing as to why the farmer did
this.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
This field forms a small hill, offering a good view of the surrounding lands.
From here, two parallel lines can be seen in the distant north. One must be the
East Road and the other has the reflective quality of a river. To the far south,
the rolling plains can be seen, until they give way to a small woody hill. East
and westwards there are but lonely farms, lost in the endless fields.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
Of course, it is a bit pretentious to call this a path as it is more a track
than a path. This particular path, which is a track, was made by the farmer and
his men as they trampled across the plain to access their fields. To the north
and the south the path quickly vanishes into the plains.
name: A Small Path

desc:
This lush green meadow is a refreshing change from the cultivated fields that
surround it. In the corner of the meadow, there is a small hearth full of cold
ashes, indicating that the farmer often comes here with his friends to eat. A
few trees provide shade from the warm sun and some singing birds makes for a
peaceful atmosphere.
name: A Picnic Place

desc:
The sharp smell of the excrements of the ponies fills this place. The stables of
the Ivy Bush Inn are busy and it seems that there is a lot of people staying at
the inn. The room is divided in stalls separated by wooden walls. Troughs are
well-filled and the animals seem to be happy. There are no windows in the walls
and the only opening is a door leading to the north, to the road before the
inn's entrance.
name: Inn Stables

desc:
There are many different types of tall bushes and small trees growing in this
area. Tiny twisting paths among the shrubs show that children from the village
in the south must quite often use this little forest as their playground.
name: Thick Bushes

desc:
Fields of wheat and corn surround this little hut which has a small, round door,
suggesting that a hobbit lives here. Outside the hut there is a bench where the
weary farmer can rest after a day's work and watch the sun set across his crops.
name: Before a Farm Hut

desc:
The simple yet carefully maintained furniture that fills this hut, tells a story
of a hobbit, who besides working hard, for a hobbit in the fields, also does lot
of work indoors. A fine collection of pipes and weed is stacked on a bookshelf
on the back wall, while two small windows with curtains on each side give a view
of the yard.
name: In a Small Hut

desc:
The field is big and full of grass. Sometimes it is even hard to walk. All
around it is silent and you can feel some animals hiding from you. Like
everything in the Shire this place is quite peaceful.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The field is quite large and filled with grass. Sometimes it is even hard to
walk. All around it is silent, and you can feel some animals hiding from you as
you draw near. Like everything in the Shire, this place is quite peaceful.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The fields here are furrowed and healthy vegetables grow in them. North the
natural fields continue, but a fence prevents you from going there. East the
cultivated fields continue.
name: Vegetable Field

desc:
The field is big and full of grass. Sometimes it is even hard to walk. All
around it is silent and you can feel some animals hiding from you. Like
everything in the Shire this place is quite peaceful.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The field is big and full of grass. Sometime it is even hard to walk. All around
it is silent and you can feel some animals hiding from you. Like everything in
the Shire this place is quite peaceful.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You are now behind the house. This is a large yard, almost a field, where the
grass doesn't grow quite well. On the ground you can see some implements for
cultivation. To the south, you see The Water flowing quietly.
name: The Backyard

desc:
Here is where the farmer comes when he is taking a rest along with his workers.
On a tiny table you can see some tools and the remainder of a recent meal.
Through the small, round windows... you can see the fields.
name: A Very Small House

desc:
The fields here are furrowed and healthy vegetables grow in them. A fence
prevents anyone from entering from the east, and leads right up to the river,
which flows quietly to the south.
name: Vegetable Field

desc:
Here, you can see a little house, in whose yard you are now. Everything seems
quite small for any race, except hobbits. Around you, you can see vegetables
growing healthily. The house is rather poor, so you think it could be just a
room for tools and implements.
name: Small Farm Yard

desc:
You are on the only path to Dwaling, which stretches straight east-west for some
distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland
and undulating hills create a scenic contrast. A small trail leads up the slope
on your south.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You are on the only path to Dwaling, which stretches straight east-west for some
distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland
and undulating hills create a scenic contrast.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:

distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland

desc:

distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland
and undulating hills create a scenic contrast. A small trail leads up the slope
on your south.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You are on the only path to Dwaling, which stretches straight east-west for some
distance. The path is not too wide, but old criss-cross tracks of wheels and
hooves suggest it is well-used. On the two sides of the path, the vast grassland
and undulating hills create a scenic contrast.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You see an endless expanse of grass extending northwards from the path to the
south, broken only by a few scattered trees. Near the path, the grass reaches
only to your ankles, but as you trudge on, more and more of your body gets
submerged in the green sea. You have no choice but to retreat to the civilized
path.
name: Grassland

desc:

You see an endless expanse of grass extending northwards from the path to the

only to your ankles, but as you trudge on, more and more of your body gets
submerged in the green sea. You have no choice but to retreat to the civilized
path.
name: Grassland

desc:
Here begins the path to Dwaling, coming out of a crossroads to the south. Ruts
and tracks crisscross the hard packed dirt, obviously well travelled and
seemingly mostly by carts from the renowned fisherhobbits of Dwaling.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
Running straight north and south by the hills of Scary, this well worn path is
somewhat muddy. The countryside around here is more hilly in the north than in
the south where there seems to be a wide flat cultivated plain.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
Bearer of many carts laden by the fish of Dwaling, this packed dirt road turns
here to the east from the south, following the lay of the land as it traces the
contour of the hills of Scary in the east.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
This road made of hard earth is the well-travelled path to Dwaling, skirting
among the foothills of Scary which rise gently to the east. The road curves
north-east from the crossroads, carving through the lovely countryside that lies
on either side.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
Here the road turns from the south and runs straight eastwards as far as the eye
can see, skirting the hills of Scary and heading directly for Dwaling. The ruts
and tracks on the road attest to its oft usage, and the hard packed dirt feels
comfortable underneath the step.
name: Path to Dwaling

desc:
You walk warily on, following the twists and turns of the tiny tunnel. With
every step, the smooth mud walls seem to edge ever closer, threatening to close
in on you.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
A small entrance is made of several oak planks with dirt pressed between the
cracks. Round windows on each side of the hole add some natural light to the
entrance. Large planks of carved oak wood can be spotted in the corners and in
the ceiling of this circular room. A small chandelier hangs down in the centre,
illuminating the entrance when natural light is insufficient. A makeshift stable
has been erected in the eastern corner, although there is not enough room to
ride for sure.
name: The Entrance

desc:
This lounge is richly decorated. Beautiful wooden figures hanging on the walls
are quite the sight for visitors in this lounge. A table is placed in the centre
of this room, small lavishly decorated chairs are lined up around the table. In
the western corner the is a small staircase with wooden carved banisters.
name: Fastolph's Lounge

desc:
A three-foot high dome occupies the centre of this grassy field. The ant-hill
looks rather old, lacking the mound of soft dirt that normally identifies a new
ant-hill. There are trees scattered about the field allowing for a soft shadow
to cross the ant-hill on a sunny afternoon. It is not obvious whether or not the
ants chose to build their home in the shade and then flourished under these
conditions.
name: An Ant-hill

desc:
The ground here is so muddy that grass has problems growing in it. The loud
noise of the river briskly flowing just north of here drown all other sounds
such as bird songs that could be heard elsewhere. To the south, the dark
straight line of a road can be seen.
name: Bank of the Water

desc:
This bedchamber is richly decorated. The wooden walls have several paintings
hanging on them, each depicting the same person. The ground is entirely covered
with a thick, flossy carpet. In the ceiling a large round mirror can be spotted.
A wooden bed has been set up in the centre of the room, filled with dried
leaves.
name: Fastolph's Master Bedroom

desc:
Right to the east lies a small, round door which leads into the hobbit hole. The
garden here seems to be well-tended. A small path leads around the grass and to
beds where flowers, vegetables, and spices for the kitchen are grown. A cut
hedge forms the dense barrier to all other directions but the east.
name: A Pretty Garden

desc:
With some effort, you have coaxed your body through the small opening. Your
sense of achievement is however short-lived, as you realise the winding tunnel
you are in shows no sign of widening at any point. Your light seems puny against
the overall darkness, your dancing shadows appear to jeer from the walls, and
altogether it is a very uncomfortable situation. You feel an urge to back out to
the pleasant fields behind.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
At one end you see the circle of light which marks the exit out of these
tunnels, and you find yourself heaving a sigh of relief. All your fears and
fancies in the depths of these tunnels seem so laughable now. You are still in
the Shire, in the old Brockenbores, and the exit is just a few steps away.
name: The Brockenbores

desc:
This hobbit home is spacious yet cosy, formed of bright wooden panels beneath
the earth. The entrance opens into a broad living room, complete with scattered
chairs and a padded couch. A pair of windows are adorned with frilled curtains,
giving a view of the peaceful Shire. An enormous tawny fur carpet covering the
floor appears first to be the coat of some great beast, but a closer glance
shows it is merely a carefully-stitched pattern of multiple fine rabbit furs.
The all-important kitchen stretches deeper into the hill, where a well-stocked
pantry is left visible behind an open door. A dragon's hoard of various tea
leaves sit shelved above the stove, while a step stool is tucked under the sink.
name: Quiet Home

desc:
Elegant wooden paneling makes this hole in the ground as opulent as any fine
home in the cities of Men (though perhaps not of Elves). Tall bookcases hold
piles of books and paperwork, and an array of armchairs sit beside an ornate
fireplace. Despite being within the hill, a narrow window is set into a wall,
which keeps the room feeling vibrant during the day as light plays over various
cheerily wistful paintings of faraway landscapes. A rounded hallway connects
this room back to the heart of the home.
name: Mergo's Studious Lounge

desc:
This quaint hobbit hole is mostly bare. A small bed sits in the corner, its
wooden frame covered in a thin layer of dust. Against one wall sits a large
bookshelf, but instead of books it is filled with maps. A few of the maps seem
to be from different places in the such a Buckland, Bywater, and the like. But
others are from much more distant lands such as Emyn-nu-Fuin, Rhun, and Anfalas.
It looks as though the owner is quite the traveller and rarely spends time at
home.
name: Shaml's Hideout
